fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies a name for the machine center.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a supplement to the machine center's name.
The Name 2 field can be used, for example, if the machine center has a long name.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the machine center's address.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.
The contents of the Address field are often used when you print out something, so you should enter the address exactly as you want it to appear.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip:
Specifies an additional line with the machine center's address.
You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.
The contents of the Address 2 field are often used when you print out something, so you should enter the address exactly as you want it to appear.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the city of the machine center's address.
How the Post Code and City Fields Are Filled In

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the machine center's post code.
How the Post Code and City Fields Are Filled In

fields.name: Work Center No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the work center to assign this machine center to.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the selected item or resource.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the center's cost that includes indirect costs, such as machine maintenance.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Queue Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the queue time of the machine center.

fields.name: Queue Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the queue time unit of measure code.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the machine center card was last modified.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether any comments have been entered for this machine center.
This field indicates whether there is a comment for this production order.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Mach. Ctr., and then click Comments. A window appears that displays existing comments and allows you to create new ones.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox. Notes follow documents when they are posted in the same way as comments.
The program automatically returns the entry to the Comment field if there is an entry in the Comments window.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.
See also the Manufacturing Comment Line table.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the capacity of the machine center.

fields.name: Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the efficiency factor as a percentage of the machine center.

fields.name: Maximum Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maximum efficiency of this machine center.

fields.name: Minimum Efficiency
fields.tooltip: Specifies the minimum efficiency of this machine center.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the machine center is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example, if the machine center is out of order.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the ledger entries for the machine center within this interval are displayed in the Capacity Ledger Entry table. The values in the quantity fields show information for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Work Shift Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a work shift filter.
If the field contains a work shift code, the values in the quantity fields are only based on the entries with the work shift code that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how they should be entered:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

1

Entries from work shift 1. (the work shift code could contain letters instead of just numbers)


Interval

1..5

Entries from work shifts 1 through 5.


Either/or

1|2

Entries that are from either work shift 1 or work shift 2.


Different from

<>1

Entries from all work shifts except work shift 1.


To see the work shift codes in the Work Shift table, click the field.

fields.name: Capacity (Total)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total available capacity of the machine center.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of this field by using the contents in the Capacity (Total) field on the Calendar Entry table.
You can filter this field so its contents are only calculated on the basis of certain dates and work shifts. The unit of measure for the Total Capacity field is the unit of measure of the attached work center.
To see the calendar entries that make up the number shown, click the field.
The program calculates the calendar entries of a machine center when you run the Calc. Machine Center Calendar batch job.

fields.name: Capacity (Effective)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the effective available capacity of the machine center.

fields.name: Prod. Order Need (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the calculated capacity requirements for production orders at this machine center.

fields.name: Prod. Order Need Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected costs, which this machine center requires to produce of the present production orders.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of this field by using the contents of the Expected Operation Cost Amt. field in the Prod. Order Routing Line table.
You can filter this field so its contents are only calculated on the basis of the status of production orders.

fields.name: Prod. Order Status Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a production order status filter.
If the field contains a production order status, the values in the fields that contain quantities are only based on entries with the production order status that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

Planned

Entries from planned production orders.


Interval

Planned..In Process

Entries from production orders within the status Planned through In Process.


Either/or

Planned|Firm Planned

Entries that are from either production orders with the status Planned or Firm Planned.


Different from

<>Simulated

Entries from all production orders except production orders with the status Simulated.


To see the production order status filters that are available, click the field.

fields.name: Setup Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long it takes to set up the machine.

fields.name: Wait Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time a job remains at the machine center after an operation is completed, until it is moved to the next operation.

fields.name: Move Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the move time required for a production lot on this machine.

fields.name: Fixed Scrap Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the fixed scrap quantity.

fields.name: Scrap %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the scrap in percent.

fields.name: Setup Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the setup time unit of measure.
You can select minutes, hours or days as the unit of measure code. For example, the setup time can be managed in minutes whereas the production time is managed in hours.
To select a unit of measure from the Capacity Unit of Measure table, click the field.

fields.name: Wait Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the wait time unit of measure.
You can select minutes, hours or days as the unit of measure code. For example, the wait time can be managed in minutes whereas the production time is managed in hours.
To select a unit of measure from the Capacity Unit of Measure table, click the field.

fields.name: Send-Ahead Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the send-ahead quantity.

fields.name: Move Time Unit of Meas. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the move time unit of measure.
You can select minutes, hours or days as the unit of measure code. For example, the move time can be managed in minutes whereas the production time is managed in hours.
To select a unit of measure from the Capacity Unit of Measure table, click the field.

fields.name: Flushing Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method to use to calculate and handle output at the machine center. Manual: Output must be posted manually by using the output journal. Forward: Output is automatically calculated and posted when you change the status of a simulated, planned (or firm planned) production order to Released. You can still post output manually from the output journal. Backward: Output is automatically calculated and posted when you change the status of a released production order to Finished. You can still post output manually from the output journal. The setting you make in this field is copied to the Flushing Method field on the production order routing line according to the machine/work center of the master routing, but you can change the field for an individual production order to allow a different output posting of that order.

fields.name: Minimum Process Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the minimum process time of the machine center.

fields.name: Maximum Process Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maximum process time of the machine center.

fields.name: Concurrent Capacities
fields.tooltip: Specifies how much available capacity must be concurrently planned for one operation at this machine center.

fields.name: Item Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies an item filter.
If the field contains an item number, the values in the quantity fields are only based on entries with the item number that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how they should be entered:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

1

Entries from item number 1. (The item number could contain letters instead of just numbers.)


Interval

1..5

Entries from item numbers 1 through 5.


Either/or

1|2

Entries that are from either item number 1 or item number 2.


Different from

<>1

Entries from all items except item number 1.


To see the item numbers in the No. table, click the field.

fields.name: Stop Code Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a stop code filter.
If the field contains a stop code, the values in the quantity fields are only based on entries with the stop code that is included in the filter.
An entry in this field enables you to quickly determine how long a machine has caused a capacity failure.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how they should be entered:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

1

Entries with stop code 1. (The stop code could contain letters instead of just numbers.)


Interval

1..5

Entries from stop codes 1 through 5.


Either/or

1|2

Entries that are from either stop code 1 or stop code 2.


Different from

<>1

Entries from all stop codes except stop code 1.


To see the stop codes in the Stop table, click the field.

fields.name: Scrap Code Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a scrap code filter.
If the field contains a scrap code, the values in the quantity fields are only based on entries with the scrap code that is included in the filter.
An entry in this field enables you to quickly determine how much material has been scrapped for a specific reason.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how they should be entered:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

1

Entries with scrap code 1. (The scrap code could contain letters instead of just numbers.)


Interval

1..5

Entries from scrap codes 1 through 5.


Either/or

1|2

Entries that are from either scrap code 1 or scrap code 2.


Different from

<>1

Entries from all scrap codes except stop code 1.


To see the scrap codes in the Scrap table, click the field.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the machine center's number.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip: Specifies the overhead rate of this machine center.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the county of the subcontractor that the machine center represents.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the subcontractor that the work center represents.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location where the machine center operates by default.

fields.name: Open Shop Floor Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin that functions as the default open shop floor bin at the work center.

fields.name: To-Production Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where components picked for production are placed by default before they can be consumed.

fields.name: From-Production Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where finished end items are taken from by default when the process involves warehouse activity.
